goal: Cut the cards.json payload for readings by serving per-language deck slices (BUG-021 follow-up / HANDOFF §2C)
done: Added scripts/generate-cards-lang.mjs (pure per-language slice of data/cards.json → data/cards-en.json ~1.5MB and data/cards-th.json ~3.2MB; shared fields in both, EN identity card_name_en/name_en/seo_slug_en force-kept in the TH slice for the EN-name-on-TH rule + EN URL construction). Rewired loadTarotData(mode) in js/data.js to fetch data/cards-<lang>.json for the current locale (?lang/?l override, else /th/ path prefix), with graceful fallback to the full cards.json if a slice is missing/invalid; per-variant localStorage cache key meowtarot_cards_full_<variant>; one-time cleanup of the legacy unsuffixed full-deck cache. card-page.js now calls loadTarotData('both') because it renders both languages in one paint (seoNotes + ?lang=both). Added a generate-cards-lang.mjs step to .github/workflows/generate-seo.yml and committed the two new data files.
root_cause: loadTarotData() always fetched the full ~4.6MB bilingual cards.json; an EN reader downloaded ~3MB of unused Thai text and vice-versa. The cards-manifest already covered the board cold-load, but the reading result still pulled the whole deck.
fix: Per-language projection + locale-aware loader. EN readers fetch 1.48MB (-68%), TH readers 3.18MB (-32%). Slice is a byte-faithful projection (verified 0 mismatches vs full deck); every runtime cross-language fallback consumer (archetype, tarot_imply, reading_summary_preview, meta_description) reads a gap-free base, so a pure slice is behavior-identical.
verified: Browser (chrome-devtools MCP, local server): EN /meanings.html auto-fetched cards-en.json; evaluate_script confirmed loadTarotData('th')->cards-th.json (156 cards, _th reading fields present, _en reading fields absent, card_name_en+seo_slug_en kept), loadTarotData('both')->cards.json (both languages), loadTarotData('en')->cards-en.json. Generator output verified as exact projection of cards.json.
affects: Reading data load for all modes (daily/question/full) + card-meaning/suit/quiz/landing pages
filed: 12 source-data cross-language gaps in cards.json (celtic_cross_environment/self/above/challenge/future/outcome, self_future, travel_past/present) where one language is filled and the other blank — backfill in cards.json to remove blanks; slice preserves today's exact (blank) behavior
iOS/www mirror sync (cp + cap sync) pending — native app falls back to full cards.json until synced (graceful, no break), so no payload savings on iOS yet
